commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.7.7: the knockout — an aperture cut through the surface plate (#30)

Recast the mask node from a generic purple/jade radial reveal into THE
KNOCKOUT: a circular aperture cut through a matte SURFACE plate (graphite,
jade ink) to uncover the SUBSTRATE plate printed beneath it (jade, dark
negative ink) — the same word system in knockout. ONE growing hole IS the
reveal; the substrate was always printed there, the aperture only uncovers
it. Deliberately distinct from zoom's loupe (no magnification, no instrument
ring) and from parallax/snow depth (no planes, no particles).

One scalar drives the knockout radius, its jade cut-edge hairline, and the
honest APERTURE-% readout from one --r on the scene root [LAW:one-source-of-truth]
— the cut edge can never leave the hole, the number can never disagree with it.

bmf feedback honored (room to breathe): beat:'dwell' holds 'the surface'
fully legible through the read-beat, tempo bumped phrase->passage, and the
cut uses a quadratic ease-IN so the surface reads on past the dwell before the
knockout accelerates — aligning the three captions (whole -> cutting ->
beneath) to the picture instead of finishing the cut while still saying
'read it whole'.

Wears the gallery's print-specimen frame (8-strip crop-mark pseudo on .mk,
positive inset for the full-bleed scene; tracked mono catalogue label; flat
matte jade, no bloom). Pure scroll-driven, no WAAPI -> no extra REDUCE_MOTION
gating beyond the global keyframe kill.

Verified in Chrome across the node's full scroll range: zero console errors,
the surface holds at r=0 through the 26% dwell, then opens with caption and
readout tracking the radius at every sample.

ticket: mit-styling-aj1.7.7
